patch.target: The real blocker isn't picking sunset beaches or poke bowls, it's getting everyone in sync on dates, reservations, and money before flights begin.
patch.before: The real blocker isn't picking sunset beaches or poke bowls, it's getting everyone in sync on dates, reservations, and money before flights begin.
patch.after: The real blocker isn't picking beaches—it's getting seven people to agree on dates before flights sell out.
patch.rationale: Humanization axis "Specific human voice vs committee" scored 6/10: Has flashes of voice ('this is the job now') but frequently slips into brand-speak and listicle cadence.

instructions.evidence: Unexpected phrasings 6/10 — Some good lines ('sand-to-terminal move') mixed with predictable travel-blog phrasing like 'delivers... crowd-pleaser food.'

patch.target: Lean into it: Duke's Waikiki delivers beachfront mai tais and crowd-pleaser food (it's touristy, and that's fine), while Rainbow Drive-In nails cheap classics if the group wanders.
patch.before: Lean into it: Duke's Waikiki delivers beachfront mai tais and crowd-pleaser food (it's touristy, and that's fine), while Rainbow Drive-In nails cheap classics if the group wanders.
patch.after: Duke's slings beachfront mai tais to people who want Duke's; Rainbow Drive-In handles anyone who'd rather eat plate lunch in a parking lot.
patch.rationale: Humanization axis "Unexpected phrasings" scored 6/10: Some good lines ('sand-to-terminal move') mixed with predictable travel-blog phrasing like 'delivers... crowd-pleaser food.'

instructions.evidence: Intro earns attention 5/10 — Buried under metadata, a stuffed TL;DR, and a typo ('sulting'); the hooky line comes third.

patch.target: TL;DR: A solid 7-day Oahu group trip itinerary maps each day to specific locations, activity intensity levels, and shared meals that prevent the logistics chaos that kills friend group trips.
patch.before: TL;DR: A solid 7-day Oahu group trip itinerary maps each day to specific locations, activity intensity levels, and shared meals that prevent the logistics chaos that kills friend group trips.
patch.after: TL;DR: Book Hanauma Bay and Diamond Head before your flights. Everything else in a friend-group Oahu week flows from those two reservations.
patch.rationale: Humanization axis "Intro earns attention" scored 5/10: Buried under metadata, a stuffed TL;DR, and a typo ('sulting'); the hooky line comes third.

instructions.evidence: specificity 6/10 — The post names real places and prices throughout (Giovanni's, Matsumoto's, $25 Hanauma Bay fee, $5 Diamond Head), which is strong. But the budget section collapses at the end into a truncated, abstract gesture — 'the stuff nobody remembers paying for' — without completing the examples. The Day 5 kayak section also references 'Kailua Surf & Sea or Enterprise Kailua' but gives no rental price range, leaving a specificity gap in an otherwise cost-conscious post. The worst offender is the cut-off final sentence.

patch.target: Budget $100 per person for the stuff nobody remembers paying for: sunblock, ABC Store snacks,
patch.before: Budget $100 per person for the stuff nobody remembers paying for: sunblock, ABC Store snacks,
patch.after: Budget $100 per person for incidentals: $18 for ABC Store sunscreen (SPF 50, 8 oz), $12 for shave ice and roadside snacks, $15 for a forgotten dry bag, and $20–30 in cash tips across plate lunch counters — it adds up fast and nobody tracks it.
patch.rationale: Quality axis "specificity" scored 6/10: The post names real places and prices throughout (Giovanni's, Matsumoto's, $25 Hanauma Bay fee, $5 Diamond Head), which is strong. But the budget section collapses at the end into a truncated, abstract gesture — 'the stuff nobody remembers paying for' — without completing the examples. The Day 5 kayak section also references 'Kailua Surf & Sea or Enterprise Kailua' but gives no rental price range, leaving a specificity gap in an otherwise cost-conscious post. The worst offender is the cut-off final sentence.
